---
title: "Public health indicators"
---

{{< include _setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _view-toggle.qmd >}}

::: {.chart-grid .view-stacked}
{{< include _charts-health.qmd >}}
:::

**About measles outbreaks.** Measles was declared eliminated in the United
States in 2000. Since then, cases have occurred primarily in unvaccinated
individuals or through importation from countries with ongoing transmission. The
2025 TX/NM outbreak was centered in a low-vaccination community in West Texas and
spread to New Mexico, the largest U.S. outbreak in recent years. The 2026 TX
outbreak peaked in January 2026, concentrated among school-age children in
counties with below-average MMR vaccination rates. The CDC recommends two doses
of the MMR vaccine for full protection; measles is highly contagious, and one
infected person can spread the virus to 9 to 18 others.


{{< include _footer.qmd >}}
